meaning: adj.分开的,分离的,个别的,单独的v.分开,隔离,分散,分别
examples: It happened on three separate occasions.
Men and women have separate exercise rooms.
Police moved in to separate the two groups.
Welsh Education Minister Leighton Andrews has ordered a separate review into the market qualifications.
Religious Jews buy only kosher-certified products and they separate their meat and dairy foods.
